name: agent-browser
tagline: CLI reference (agent-browser) for driving a browser: navigate, click, fill forms, screenshot, extract page data.
verdict: Clean, well-scoped CLI reference from vercel-labs with real safety knobs (--allowed-domains, --action-policy, --confirm-actions, encrypted local auth vault that keeps passwords from the LLM). Minor risks: `eval -b` runs base64-encoded JS on pages, which can hide payload intent from a quick read; no version/changelog signal; no guidance on command failures or

highlights.summary: `agent-browser eval -b "base64code" # Run base64-encoded JS` (line 226) lets arbitrary JS be passed encoded, which can obscure what a page-eval command actually does from a quick review.
No statement of when NOT to use the skill (e.g., non-browser tasks, when a simpler HTTP fetch suffices).
No guidance on handling failures — e.g., what to do if an element ref is stale, a wait times out, or a command errors.
